title: Bulk report - Week 18
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market has made the most of the short working week as rates have lifted strongly, with the 5TC now residing at $24,002 - up $6262 week on week. The positive market sentiment was largely Pacific led yet that didn't stop the Atlantic Basin and ballaster trades from getting in on the act, as all regions posted big gains. Cargo levels in the north Atlantic are heard to be poor yet a wide range of rate levels have been mentioned as the market struggles for visibility in the region as usual.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market has made the most of the short working week as rates have lifted strongly, with the 5TC now residing at $24,002 - up $6262 week on week. The positive market sentiment was largely Pacific led yet that didn't stop the Atlantic Basin and ballaster trades from getting in on the act, as all regions posted big gains.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: A midweek physical and FFA surge gave the Panamax market some much needed impetus following a few fragmented weeks owing to holidays. Improved demand from the Americas proved to be a key catalyst to gains in most markets this week as EC South America grain impacted the gains seen both for tonnage open ex Mediterranean and Southeast Asia.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: With many holidays over the last week, the sector had a rather lacklustre feel with limited fresh enquiry in a few areas. In the Atlantic brokers said the US Gulf remained fairly strong and some said there was an increase in demand from the Continent/Mediterranean regions.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: In a week full of holidays and limited visible trading the BHSI retained a positive trend in both basins. The biggest moves came in East Coast South America with a 37,000-dwt fixing from Vitoria to the Continent with an intended cargo of pig iron at $40,000 and a 38,000-dwt fixing from Santos to the United Kingdom with an intended cargo of Sugar at $42,000.
